chunk_summary: This section focuses on tele-follow-up and remote adherence verification for managing feeding difficulties and faltering growth, emphasizing integration with community programs and leveraging digital tools for monitoring adherence and outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of tele-follow-up in managing feeding difficulties and faltering growth.
Learn methods for verifying adherence to nutritional and medical interventions remotely.
Explore integration of community programs like ICDS and NHM for comprehensive care.
Identify key guidelines for monitoring growth and adherence using digital tools.
key_facts: Faltering growth is recognized and managed under NICE NG75 guidelines.
The IMNCI chart booklet provides standardized protocols for child health monitoring.
The Mother and Child Protection (MCP) card is a critical tool for tracking growth and adherence in community settings.
The ICDS scheme integrates tele-follow-up with community-based interventions for child health.
Remote adherence verification can improve compliance with feeding and nutritional guidelines.
reference_quote: Faltering growth: recognition and management (NICE NG75) provides evidence-based guidelines for identifying and addressing growth concerns in children.
